articles.title: Türkiye's 120 GW renewable target puts grid modernisation and aluminium demand in focus
articles.summary: Turkey is targeting 120 GW of combined solar and wind capacity by 2035, backed by approximately $80 billion in planned renewable investment; aluminium's role in electrical cables, power transmission lines, and solar mounting structures is identified as a key downstream demand driver.
articles.novelis_angle: Turkey's renewable infrastructure buildout represents an emerging demand signal for industrial and specialty aluminium sheet in Novelis's European supply geography, particularly for electrical and construction-grade product lines.

articles.title: UK aluminium scrap exports grow 9% in Jan–Apr 2026 despite declines to India, Hong Kong, Germany and China
articles.summary: UK aluminium scrap exports grew 9% year-on-year in January–April 2026, despite falling volumes to India, Hong Kong, Germany, and China; the shift reflects trade flow diversification by UK exporters amid evolving EU and UK scrap export policy.
articles.novelis_angle: Rising UK scrap export volumes and the shift away from traditional markets tighten domestic secondary aluminium availability, affecting feedstock supply and input costs for Novelis's Latchford beverage can recycling facility.

articles.title: TOMRA expands aluminium alloy sorting system AUTOSORT PULSE to heavy metals, IBA and magnesium
articles.summary: TOMRA has extended its Dynamic LIBS-based AUTOSORT PULSE sorting system beyond aluminium alloy identification to cover stainless steel, heavy metals, magnesium, and incinerator bottom ash (IBA) streams, expanding the technology's applicability in mixed-materials processing facilities.

articles.title: Adani and IHC unveil $11.5 billion Odisha aluminium complex with 4–5 year timeline
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises and Abu Dhabi's IHC (International Resources Holding) signed a $11.5 billion 50:50 JV MoU to build India's largest aluminium complex in Odisha — comprising a 4 Mt/yr alumina refinery, a 2 Mt/yr primary smelter, and a 1 Mt/yr downstream park — representing the largest-ever foreign direct investment in the Indian metals sector.
articles.novelis_angle: Adding approximately 2 Mt/yr of Indian primary capacity within 4–5 years would meaningfully shift the global aluminium supply balance and could exert long-term downward pressure on P1020 premiums, a key input cost for Novelis's European smelter-free FRP operations.
